title: Contributor Guidelines
text: core branches used repository : main: Contains production version package (protected). {issue#}: Contains code specific issue, named issue number prefix.

title: Contributor Guidelines
text: Describe development step GitHub issue. Create new branch main named issue number prefix (e.g. 42-add-pca-tab). Use test-driven development (TDD): write test first, write code pass test. GitHub Actions must pass merging branch main.

title: Contributor Guidelines
text: Code formatted {styler} release. can trigger styler PR commenting /style (available repository members).

title: Contributor Guidelines
text: CI enforces 100 % line coverage (covr::percent_coverage() == 100). Every line R code must either exercised test explicitly excluded # nocov annotation.

title: Contributor Guidelines
text: Pure (non-Shiny) utility plotting functions — write test instead. Branches can reached constructing right data frame test. New exported functions — every exported function must least one test. adding # nocov, keep excluded region small possible prefer inline # nocov start/end blocks single lines.

title: Contributor Guidelines
text: exported functions must roxygen2 documentation. can regenerate documentation PR commenting /document (available repository members). Roxygen import tags centralized R/ctasapp-pkg.R.

title: User Guide
text: ctasapp Shiny application exploring results ctas (Clinical Timeseries Anomaly Spotter) R package. data pipeline : Input_*() helper returns list four elements:
code: Raw SDTM domains (dm, lb, vs, rs_onco)   |   v Input_*() helpers               -- transform to ctas input + keep untransformed originals   |   v ctas::process_a_study()         -- compute site-level outlier scores   |   v ctasapp (Shiny visualization)   -- interactive exploration of scores, plots, and data

title: User Guide
text: data element following columns: parameters element defines parameter: parameter_category_3 value determines parameter visualized:

title: User Guide
code: library(pharmaversesdtm) data("dm"); data("lb")  labs <- ctasapp::Input_Labs(dm, lb) str(labs, max.level = 1) List of 5  $ data          : tibble [82,918 x 7]  $ subjects      : tibble [254 x 4]  $ parameters    : tibble [78 x 5]  $ untransformed : tibble [46,164 x 6] head(labs$data[labs$data$parameter_id == "LB_NORM_ALT", ]) subject_id parameter_id timepoint_rank timepoint_1_name timepoint_2_name baseline    result   AB12345-01 LB_NORM_ALT              1       SCREENING 1             NA       NA  0.6363636   AB12345-01 LB_NORM_ALT              2            WEEK 2             NA       NA  0.5454545   ... head(labs$parameters) parameter_id      parameter_name parameter_category_1 parameter_category_2 parameter_category_3   LB_NORM_ALT   ALT (norm)         Labs                 ALT                  range_normalized   LB_MISS_ALT   ALT (missing)      Labs                 ALT                  ratio_missing   LB_NORM_ALKPH ALKPH (norm)        Labs                 ALKPH                range_normalized   LB_MISS_ALKPH ALKPH (missing)     Labs                 ALKPH                ratio_missing   ...

title: User Guide
text: normalize_by_range() transforms lab values 0–1 scale using reference ranges. Values within normal range fall 0 1; -range values negative, -range greater 1. result=value−lowerupper−lower\text{result} = \frac{\text{value} - \text{lower}}{\text{upper} - \text{lower}} Input_Labs() applies lab parameter, producing rows parameter_id = "LB_NORM_<LBTESTCD>" parameter_category_3 = "range_normalized".
code: value <- c(15, 30, 45, 60) lower <- rep(10, 4) upper <- rep(50, 4)  normalize_by_range(value, lower, upper) #> [1] 0.125 0.500 0.875 1.250

title: User Guide
text: ratio_missing_over_time() computes running proportion missing (NA) values per subject parameter. timepoint kk, value : ratiok=∑=1k𝟏[valuei=NA]k\text{ratio}_k = \frac{\sum_{=1}^{k} \mathbf{1}[\text{value}_i = \text{NA}]}{k} subject missing values always ratio 0. One values missing ratio 1. Input_Labs() produces rows parameter_id = "LB_MISS_<LBTESTCD>" parameter_category_3 = "ratio_missing". missingness parameter shares parameter_category_2 range-normalized sibling (e.g. LB_NORM_ALT LB_MISS_ALT parameter_category_2 = "ALT"). app, displayed faceted pair: upper facet shows normalized values, lower shows missingness ratio.
code: value <- c(10, NA, 30, NA, 50) subject_id <- rep("subj_1", 5) parameter_id <- rep("ALT", 5)  ratio_missing_over_time(value, subject_id, parameter_id) #> [1] 0.0000000 0.5000000 0.3333333 0.5000000 0.4000000

title: User Guide
text: encode_categorical() one-hot encodes categorical variable long format. observation expanded one row per category level, encoded = 1 observation matches level encoded = 0 otherwise. Input_RS() uses transform RSORRES values. resulting parameter_id values "RS_OVRLRESP=CR", "RS_OVRLRESP=PD", etc., parameter_category_3 = "categorical". app renders alluvial plots showing response categories flow across visits.
code: responses <- c("CR", "PR", "SD", "PD", "CR", "SD") encoded <- encode_categorical(responses, prefix = "RS_OVRLRESP") head(encoded, 10) #>    orig_row          level encoded #> 1         1 RS_OVRLRESP=CR       1 #> 2         2 RS_OVRLRESP=CR       0 #> 3         3 RS_OVRLRESP=CR       0 #> 4         4 RS_OVRLRESP=CR       0 #> 5         5 RS_OVRLRESP=CR       1 #> 6         6 RS_OVRLRESP=CR       0 #> 7         1 RS_OVRLRESP=PD       0 #> 8         2 RS_OVRLRESP=PD       0 #> 9         3 RS_OVRLRESP=PD       0 #> 10        4 RS_OVRLRESP=PD       1

title: User Guide
text: Input_BMI() creates screening-weight category vital signs. Weights binned categories (< 60 kg, 60-80 kg, 80-100 kg, >= 100 kg), one-hot encoded like Input_RS. single timepoint per subject, parameter_category_3 = "bar" signals app render bar chart rather alluvial plot.
code: library(pharmaversesdtm) data("dm"); data("vs")  bmi <- ctasapp::Input_BMI(dm, vs) head(bmi$parameters) parameter_id                parameter_name parameter_category_1 parameter_category_2 parameter_category_3   VS_WEIGHT_CAT=60-80 kg     60-80 kg       Vital Signs          VS_WEIGHT_CAT        bar   VS_WEIGHT_CAT=80-100 kg    80-100 kg      Vital Signs          VS_WEIGHT_CAT        bar   VS_WEIGHT_CAT=>= 100 kg    >= 100 kg      Vital Signs          VS_WEIGHT_CAT        bar   VS_WEIGHT_CAT=< 60 kg      < 60 kg        Vital Signs          VS_WEIGHT_CAT        bar

title: User Guide
text: Input_VS() passes vital-sign measurements without transformation. raw VSSTRESN values become result, parameter_category_3 = "numeric". rendered simple line plots app.
code: library(pharmaversesdtm) data("dm"); data("vs")  vs_input <- ctasapp::Input_VS(dm, vs) head(vs_input$data[vs_input$data$parameter_id == "VS_SYSBP", ]) subject_id parameter_id timepoint_rank timepoint_1_name result   AB12345-01 VS_SYSBP              1       SCREENING 1      132   AB12345-01 VS_SYSBP              2       BASELINE          128   AB12345-01 VS_SYSBP              3       WEEK 2            130   ...

title: User Guide
text: package bundles pre-computed sample data (sample_sdtm_data sample_sdtm_results) derived pharmaversesdtm. can used directly generate plot types.

what: Range-normalized lab values with missingness ratio
title: User Guide
text: ALT parameter LB_NORM_ALT (range-normalized) LB_MISS_ALT (missingness ratio). plotted together faceted pair.

title: User Guide
text: Systolic blood pressure (VS_SYSBP) uses raw numeric values without normalization.

title: User Guide
text: Overall response (RS_OVRLRESP) categories displayed alluvial plot showing transitions across visits.

title: User Guide
text: Screening weight categories displayed bar chart since one timepoint per subject.
code: bar_ids <- unique(m$parameter_id[grepl("^VS_WEIGHT_CAT=", m$parameter_id)]) plot_bar(bar_ids, m, thresh = 1.3) #> Warning: There were 32 warnings in `dplyr::mutate()`. #> The first warning was: #> ℹ In argument: `ci95_low = purrr::map2_dbl(...)`. #> Caused by warning in `stats::prop.test()`: #> ! Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect #> ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 31 remaining warnings.

title: User Guide
text: app supports uploading custom data via 2 mandatory 2 optional flat files (CSV, Parquet, RDA): See collapsible “File format documentation” panel app’s Data tab full column specifications. optional study column Input file enables multi-study filtering Fields panel.

title: User Guide
text: Use generate_sample_csv() create set example CSV files bundled sample data. useful trying upload feature template data: writes results.csv, input.csv, untransformed.csv, queries.csv specified directory. files combine sample datasets (sample_ctas_data STUDY-001 sample_sdtm_data STUDY-002) multi-study upload example.

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {ctasapp: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output},   author = {Björn Koneswarakantha},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0}, }

what: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
title: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
text: Interactive Shiny application exploring results {ctas} (Clinical Timeseries Anomaly Spotter) R package. Provides drill-visualizations site-level outlier scores subject-level time series data.

title: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
text: can install development version ctasapp GitHub :

title: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
text: Launch app bundled sample data: app running, click Use Sample Data Data tab, switch Fields tab explore parameter-level timeseries site outlier scores.

title: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
text: app ships default config.yml controls colours, parameter icons, ctas features pre-selected. customise, copy default config pass version run_ctas_app(): config file YAML three sections: key omit falls back built-default.
code: # Copy the shipped default as a starting point file.copy(   system.file("config.yml", package = "ctasapp"),   "my_config.yml" )  # Edit my_config.yml to taste, then launch: run_ctas_app(config = "my_config.yml") colors:   score_breaks: [1.3, 3, 5, 10]   plot: ["#9ED782", "#fed8019c", "#fed801", "#FEAA01", "#FF5858"]   table: ["#FFFFFF", "#feed01", "#fed801", "#FEAA01", "#FF5858"]   table_text: ["#1A1A1A", "#1A1A1A", "#1A1A1A", "#FFFFFF", "#FFFFFF"]   query_no_change: "#a380e9"   query_data_change: "#2790e0"  icons:   range_normalized: flask   numeric: chart-line   categorical: water   bar: chart-bar  features:   default:     - autocorr     - average     - sd

title: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
text: package documentation developed assistance AI tools, including Cursor Claude Opus 4.6. AI-generated content reviewed.

title: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
text: Since {ctas} designed use GCP framework, conducted extensive quality control part development process. particular, following early development: Unit Tests - Unit tests written core functions, 100% coverage required. Lines tested non-interactive context (e.g. Shiny reactive inputs, render callbacks, defensive guards fire inside running app) annotated # nocov excluded coverage metric. See Contributor Guidelines full policy. Workflow Tests - shiny::testServer() tests verify Shiny module logic behaves expected. Function Documentation - Detailed documentation exported function maintained Roxygen. Package Checks - Standard package checks run using GitHub Actions must passing PRs merged. Continuous Integration - Continuous integration provided via GitHub Actions. Code Formatting - Code formatted {styler} release. Contributor Guidelines - Contributor guidelines including step--step processes code development provided vignette.

title: Shiny App to Visualize ctas Output
text: development progresses, also conduct following quality control steps: Qualification Workflow - assessments Qualified described Qualification Workflow Vignette. Qualification Report Vignette generated attached release. Code Review - Code review conducted using GitHub Pull Requests (PRs), log PRs included Qualification Report Vignette. Regression Testing - Extensive QC testing done release. End--End Tests - Browser-based tests using {shinytest2} validate full user interaction flow.

title: Aggregate uploaded results by removing timeseries_id — aggregate_results

what: Arrange visit names into clinically meaningful order — arrange_timepoints
title: Arrange visit names into clinically meaningful order — arrange_timepoints
text: Orders factor levels: screening first, baseline, remaining visits natural numeric order ("WEEK 2" < "WEEK 12"), discontinuation last. Uses sort_visits_natural() middle group avoid lexical misordering numbered visit names.

title: Arrange visit names into clinically meaningful order — arrange_timepoints
Arrange visit names into clinically meaningful order — arrange_timepoints
text: x Character vector visit names.

title: Arrange visit names into clinically meaningful order — arrange_timepoints
text: factor levels ordered: screening, baseline, numbered visits, discontinuation.

what: Build display parameter lookup from measures — build_param_lookup
title: Build display parameter lookup from measures — build_param_lookup
text: Groups parameters parameter_category_2 ("field" key). naturally groups norm + missing-ratio labs single entry, categorical one-hot levels shared prefix. plot_type determined dominant parameter_category_3; mixed types (e.g. range_normalized + ratio_missing) treated "numeric".

title: Build display parameter lookup from measures — build_param_lookup
Build display parameter lookup from measures — build_param_lookup
text: df Measures data frame.

title: Build display parameter lookup from measures — build_param_lookup
text: data frame display_id, parameter_ids (list column), plot_type, cat3_values (list column).

title: Build display parameter lookup from measures — build_param_lookup
text: multiple parameters share parameter_category_2 identical parameter_category_3 values meant grouped (e.g. two independent numeric params generic category), kept separate using parameter_id display key. Now also includes cat3_values list column raw parameter_category_3 values icon determination.

title: Field Detail Module - Server — mod_FieldDetail_server
text: Renders clickable list parameters sidebar. selection, shows pill-tabbed score tables (regular + missingness), timeseries plot, raw data table outlier sites. Auto-detects plot type parameter_category_3. Supports feature sub-selection via checkbox group.

title: Field Detail Module - Server — mod_FieldDetail_server
code: mod_FieldDetail_server(   id,   rctv_measures,   rctv_ctas_results,   rctv_untransformed = shiny::reactiveVal(NULL),   rctv_queries = shiny::reactiveVal(NULL),   rctv_dataset_label = shiny::reactiveVal(NULL),   rctv_studies = shiny::reactiveVal(NULL) )

title: Field Detail Module - Server — mod_FieldDetail_server
text: id Module namespace ID. rctv_measures Reactive expression returning measures data frame. rctv_ctas_results Reactive expression returning raw ctas results list. rctv_untransformed Reactive expression returning untransformed timeseries data frame (NULL ctas sample data). rctv_queries Reactive expression returning query data frame (NULL queries available). rctv_dataset_label Reactive expression returning dataset label string (e.g. "ctas sample", "SDTM sample", user filename). rctv_studies Reactive expression returning character vector available study names, NULL data study column one study.

title: Score colour constants — score_colors
text: Breakpoints colour vectors DT table styling ggplot2 plot colouring. -threshold uses soft green; outlier bands graduate yellow red. SCORE_COLORS_TABLE uses white first band yellow--red outliers. SCORE_COLORS_TABLE_TEXT provides foreground colours DT cells. Query dots use light purple (change) blue (data change). can overridden runtime via apply_config().
